steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: The claim: Newland "compares his intellect favorably to that of other men" — i.e., he thinks he is intellectually superior to the other men in the audience.

steps.title: Match the quotation to the claim
steps.content:
Choice B states Newland "felt himself distinctly the superior of these chosen specimens... he had probably read more, thought more, and even seen a good deal more of the world." This directly illustrates a favorable intellectual comparison.

A is about life going on; C is about the opera setting; D actually says he accepts the men's views — the opposite of feeling superior.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
